mechanism_narrative: The 3-month trend in industrial production transmits directly to near-term earnings expectations; a negative INDPRO trend at the decision date predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: series:INDPRO
fact:latest INDPRO snapshot value is 107.254 as of t-2mo
failure_condition: Reject if negative 3-month INDPRO momentum is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of walk-forward observations.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in unemployment signals deteriorating household income and weaker corporate revenue expectations; when UNRATE crosses a recession threshold, S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are negative.
evidence_refs: series:UNRATE
fact:latest UNRATE snapshot value is 6.5 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE crossings above 6.0% produce average 20-day S&P 500 returns greater than the unconditional average in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Aggressive federal funds rate cuts lower discount rates and signal policy support; when FEDFUNDS crosses below 1.0%, the policy impulse predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: series:FEDFUNDS
fact:latest FEDFUNDS snapshot value is 0.97 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if FEDFUNDS crossings below 1.0% are followed by nonpositive average 60-day S&P 500 returns or a hit rate below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales weakness combined with rising unemployment creates a consumption-labor feedback loop that reduces expected earnings; the joint signal predicts negative 20-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month growth crosses below 0 and UNRATE rises more than 0.3pp over 3 months
evidence_refs: series:RSAFS
series:UNRATE
fact:latest RSAFS snapshot value is 363696 as of t-1mo
fact:latest UNRATE snapshot value is 6.5 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if the joint RSAFS-UNRATE trigger has no lower 20-day return mean than either single-series trigger in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts below a crisis threshold indicate collapsing construction, household wealth pressure, and mortgage-credit losses; HOUST below 900 predicts negative 60-day S&P 500 returns.
evidence_refs: series:HOUST
fact:latest HOUST snapshot value is 817 as of t-2mo
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST crossings below 900 are followed by positive average 60-day S&P 500 returns or if the signal hit rate is below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Disinflation combined with falling policy rates raises real liquidity and reduces input-cost pressure; when CPIAUCSL momentum turns negative while FEDFUNDS falls, S&P 500 returns turn positive over 20 days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month change crosses below 0 and FEDFUNDS falls more than 100bp over 3 months
evidence_refs: series:CPIAUCSL
series:FEDFUNDS
fact:latest CPIAUCSL snapshot value is 218.813 as of t-2mo
fact:latest FEDFUNDS snapshot value is 0.97 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if the joint CPIAUCSL-FEDFUNDS trigger produces negative average 20-day returns or fails to outperform FEDFUNDS-only easing signals.

mechanism_narrative: Abrupt retail sales contractions cause immediate revenue downgrades for cyclical and consumer firms; when RSAFS monthly growth crosses below minus 1%, S&P 500 returns are negative over 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: series:RSAFS
fact:latest RSAFS snapshot value is 363696 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if RSAFS monthly growth crossings below -1.0% do not produce negative average 5-day S&P 500 returns after release-date alignment.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll contraction with emergency-rate policy signals recession stress dominating liquidity support; when PAYEMS falls while FEDFUNDS is below 2%, S&P 500 returns are negative over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change crosses below 0 while FEDFUNDS is below 2.0%
evidence_refs: series:PAYEMS
series:FEDFUNDS
fact:latest PAYEMS snapshot value is 136899 as of t-1mo
fact:latest FEDFUNDS snapshot value is 0.97 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if PAYEMS contraction under FEDFUNDS below 2.0% is followed by positive average 60-day returns or underperforms no-interaction payroll signals.
